proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib.
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib-treated patients have markedly longer PFS: 5.41 vs 4.31 months (n=17,428 vs 32,572).
Tamoxifen-treated have nearly identical PFS to untreated: 4.72 vs 4.68 months.
Trastuzumab-treated have a small reduction in PFS: 4.65 vs 4.71 months.
Olaparib-treated and untreated have indistinguishable PFS: 4.66 vs 4.70 months.
Sacituzumab-govitecan main effect on PFS is essentially zero: 4.69 vs 4.69 months.
Pembrolizumab-treated patients have a small reduction in PFS overall: 4.63 vs 4.70 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_tamoxifen is associated with longer pfs_months than no tamoxifen.
Within her2_positive=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no trastuzumab.
Within brca1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib is associated with longer pfs_months than no olaparib.
In patients with any BRCA1/2 mutation, treatment_olaparib produces longer pfs_months than no olaparib (interaction effect).

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by tamoxifen, restricted to er_positive==1
analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen does not extend PFS in the ER-positive subgroup (4.88 vs 4.86 months, n=10,535 vs 24,351).

analyses.code: Welch t-test pfs_months by trastuzumab in her2_positive==1
analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab does not extend PFS in HER2-positive patients (4.25 vs 4.33 months, n=1,890 vs 7,170); direction is mildly negative.

analyses.code: Welch t-test pfs_months by olaparib in brca1_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA1-mut subset trends positive (5.03 vs 4.63 months) but underpowered (n=120 vs 1,157).

analyses.code: Welch t-test pfs_months by olaparib in brca2_mutation==1
analyses.result_summary: Similar positive direction in BRCA2-mut: 4.84 vs 4.54 months (n=119 vs 1,103); not significant.

analyses.code: Welch t-test pfs_months by olaparib in (brca1_mutation|brca2_mutation)==1
analyses.result_summary: Pooling BRCA1/2 mutations (n=2,499) the olaparib benefit reaches significance: 4.93 vs 4.59 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months substantially more in er_positive=1 patients than in er_positive=0 patients.
treatment_palbociclib provides little or no PFS benefit in her2_positive=1 patients compared with her2_positive=0.
treatment_palbociclib benefit is attenuated or abolished in pik3ca_mutation=1 patients relative to pik3ca_mutation=0.
Within HR+/HER2- (er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0), treatment_palbociclib produces a large positive PFS effect (~+2 months).
Within HR-/HER2+ and TNBC subsets, treatment_palbociclib produces no PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib effect stratified by er_positive
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib raises PFS by +1.58 months in ER-positive (5.89 vs 4.31; n=12,194 vs 22,692) but only -0.02 in ER-negative (n=5,234).

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib effect stratified by her2_positive
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib raises PFS by +1.35 in HER2-negative (5.66 vs 4.31) but is null (-0.01) in HER2-positive.

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib effect stratified by pik3ca_mutation
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib effect is +1.66 in PIK3CA wild-type (5.97 vs 4.31; n=11,405 vs 21,230) and only +0.04 in PIK3CA-mutant.

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib effect within HR+/HER2- (er_positive==1 & her2_positive==0)
analyses.result_summary: In HR+/HER2- (n=28,589) palbociclib raises PFS by +1.92 months (6.24 vs 4.31).

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib in HR-/HER2+ and in TNBC
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib has no benefit in HR-/HER2+ (-0.09; n=992 vs 1,771) or in TNBC (0.00; n=4,242 vs 8,109).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months versus no pembrolizumab.
Within TNBC (er_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0), treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months versus no pembrolizumab.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs between has_brain_mets=1 and has_brain_mets=0.
treatment_pembrolizumab has no positive PFS effect in any major breast cancer subgroup tested (MSI-H, TNBC, ER+, HER2+, brain mets, stage IV).

analyses.result_summary: Among MSI-H (n=86 tx vs 412 ctrl) pembrolizumab trends mildly positive (4.84 vs 4.70) but is not significant.
Within TNBC (n=1,878 tx vs 10,473 ctrl) pembrolizumab effect is essentially null (4.33 vs 4.29).

analyses.code: Welch t-test pembrolizumab stratified by has_brain_mets
analyses.result_summary: In brain-mets patients pembrolizumab effect is -0.12 (NS); in no-brain-mets it is -0.07 (p=0.035). No positive subgroup found.

analyses.code: Stratified Welch t-tests across er_positive, her2_positive, msi_high, tp53_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, stage_iv, has_brain_mets
analyses.result_summary: Across all tested subgroups pembrolizumab effect is null or slightly negative; the only nominally significant differences are negative (e.g., -0.10 in ER+, p=0.005; -0.10 in tp53_mut, p=0.029).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months relative to no sacituzumab.
Within her2_low=1 patients, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months relative to no sacituzumab.
Within her2_low=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases pfs_months relative to no trastuzumab.
Within her2_amplification=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab increases pfs_months relative to no trastuzumab.

analyses.result_summary: Sacituzumab in TNBC: 4.22 vs 4.31 months (n=1,254 vs 11,097), trend negative not significant.
Sacituzumab in HER2-low: 4.74 vs 4.77 (n=1,833 vs 16,710); null.
Trastuzumab in HER2-low: 4.75 vs 4.77 months; null.
Trastuzumab in HER2-amplified (n=302 vs 1,165): 4.73 vs 4.76, null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic decrease).
Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
node_positive, liver_mets, bone_mets, and pleural_effusion are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV cuts PFS by ~1.5 months (3.62 vs 5.15; n=14,969 vs 35,031).
Brain mets cut PFS by ~1.0 month (3.75 vs 4.80).

analyses.code: Spearman ECOG vs pfs_months and stratified means
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS falls monotonically with ECOG: 0->5.64, 1->4.44, 2->3.29 (Spearman rho=-0.334).
Strikingly strong POSITIVE association (rho=+0.726): older patients have longer PFS. Decile means rise from 1.7 (age 30-52) to 7.8 months (age 78-90).
Higher albumin associated with longer PFS (rho=+0.102, p~1e-115).
Greater weight loss linked to shorter PFS (rho=-0.116).
Higher Ki67 modestly associated with shorter PFS (rho=-0.083).
Higher LDH weakly associated with shorter PFS (rho=-0.013, p=0.0046).
Tumor size has no association with PFS (rho=-0.002, p=0.63).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for node_positive, liver_mets, bone_mets, pleural_effusion
analyses.result_summary: None of these classical 'bad' features predict shorter PFS in this cohort (all |diff|<0.04, p>0.1).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories.
rural_residence=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than urban patients.
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
sex_female=1 patients have different pfs_months than sex_female=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: No race_ethnicity differences (F=0.85, p=0.49); group means fall in 4.68-4.76 months.
No insurance differences (F=0.31, p=0.82).
Rural vs urban PFS 4.71 vs 4.69; null.
Null association (rho=-0.006, p=0.20).
Null association (rho=-0.003, p=0.55).
No PFS difference between male (4.70) and female (4.69) patients despite this being a 'breast' cohort with 55% male.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, Ki67 and tumor size, treatment_palbociclib remains independently associated with longer PFS while the other treatments do not.

age_years (positive), ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin (positive), ldh, weight_loss, ki67, er_positive (positive), her2_positive (negative), and pik3ca_mutation (negative) are each independent predictors of pfs_months in a multivariable OLS.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + brain_mets + ER + HER2 + her2_low + PIK3CA + BRCA1 + BRCA2 + TP53 + MSI + 6 treatments + albumin + LDH + weight_loss + ki67 + tumor_size + prior_lines
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.79. Adjusted coefficients: age +0.176/yr (p<1e-300), ECOG -1.17/level, stage_iv -1.55, brain_mets -0.98, ER+ +0.54, HER2+ -0.47, PIK3CA -0.58, albumin +0.48, LDH -5e-4, weight_loss -0.077, ki67 -0.019, palbociclib +1.10 (all p<1e-30); tamoxifen -0.008 (NS), trastuzumab -0.010 (NS), olaparib -0.026 (NS), pembrolizumab -0.020 (NS), sacituzumab +0.033 (borderline p=0.05). Tumor size, BRCA2, TP53, MSI, HER2-low, prior_lines all NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant positive interaction between treatment_palbociclib and er_positive on pfs_months (palbociclib benefit is larger when ER+).
There is a significant negative interaction between treatment_palbociclib and her2_positive on pfs_months (palbociclib benefit is reduced when HER2+).
There is a significant negative interaction between treatment_palbociclib and pik3ca_mutation on pfs_months (PIK3CA mutation abolishes palbociclib benefit).
There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and BRCA1/2 mutation status on pfs_months.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_tamoxifen and er_positive on pfs_months.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_trastuzumab and her2_positive on pfs_months.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and msi_high on pfs_months.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan and her2_low on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS with palbociclib*er_positive interaction adjusted for age/ECOG/stage/albumin/brain_mets
analyses.result_summary: Highly significant positive interaction: +1.55 months (p<1e-300) on top of palbociclib main +0.83.
Highly significant negative interaction: -1.32 months (p<1e-300).
Highly significant negative interaction: -1.65 months (p<1e-300). PIK3CA mutation effectively abolishes palbociclib benefit.
Olaparib*BRCA1 +0.23 (p=0.094); olaparib*BRCA2 +0.02 (p=0.89). Directional positive but not significant.
No interaction: tamoxifen:er_positive coef=-0.010 (p=0.75); tamoxifen:postmenopausal coef=-0.013 (p=0.65).
All interactions null: trastuzumab:her2_positive +0.014 (p=0.74), trastuzumab:her2_low -0.018 (p=0.60), trastuzumab:her2_amplification -0.082 (p=0.37).
Pembrolizumab:msi_high +0.19 (p=0.25); pembrolizumab:tnbc +0.06 (p=0.15). Directional but not significant.
Sacituzumab:her2_low +0.048 (p=0.27); sacituzumab:tnbc -0.04 (p=0.39). Both null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 24 SNP indicators is associated with pfs_months at the Bonferroni-corrected threshold (alpha = 0.05/24 = 0.00208).

analyses.code: Welch t-test for each snp_* main effect on pfs_months; Bonferroni correction over 24 tests
analyses.result_summary: Best SNP is snp_rs1801131 (mean diff -0.054, p=0.026), well above the corrected threshold of 2.08e-3. None of the 24 SNPs is significant after correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Major comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, autoimmune_disease, hepatitis_b/c, hiv_positive, prior_malignancy) are each associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher symptom burden (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, appetite_loss_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Vital signs (systolic_bp_mmhg, diastolic_bp_mmhg, heart_rate_bpm, spo2_pct, bmi) correlate with pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for 19 comorbidities and metastatic site indicators
analyses.result_summary: All 19 comorbidities and additional metastatic-site flags are null (|diff|<0.10 mo, all p>0.15). Includes hiv_positive (-0.10, p=0.36), hepatitis_b (-0.09, p=0.29), prior_malignancy (-0.05, p=0.17).

analyses.code: Spearman rho across five symptom grades
analyses.result_summary: All symptom grades have |rho|<=0.004 and p>0.4. No prognostic signal in patient-reported symptoms.

analyses.code: Spearman rho across five vital signs
analyses.result_summary: All vital signs |rho|<=0.007 and p>0.13. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the rare alteration features (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret/ros1/ntrk/nrg1 fusions, braf_v600e, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, keap1_mutation, pten_loss, msi_high) has an independent main effect on pfs_months.
treatment_palbociclib benefit is preserved across stage_iv and brain_mets strata (i.e., no interaction with these clinical risk factors).
treatment_palbociclib benefit is modestly attenuated in older age (negative palbociclib*age interaction).

analyses.result_summary: All effects small (|diff|<=0.22) and none significant after multiple testing. The largest nominally directional signals are negative for met_exon14_skipping (-0.14, p=0.024) and fgfr_alteration (-0.15, p=0.073), but neither survives Bonferroni.
Both interactions null: palbociclib:stage_iv coef=+0.022 (p=0.39); palbociclib:brain_mets coef=-0.052 (p=0.19). Palbociclib benefit is preserved across stages.
Small negative palbociclib*age interaction (-0.0026/yr, p=0.033) but the absolute palbociclib benefit remains large at all ages. Palbociclib*ECOG NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Number of concurrent treatments is positively correlated with pfs_months (additive treatment effects).
Within ER-positive patients, the combination of palbociclib + tamoxifen produces longer PFS than palbociclib alone (synergy).

analyses.code: Spearman pfs_months ~ count of treatment_* flags
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS rises from 4.32 (0 tx) to 4.69, 4.91, 5.11, 5.26 with 1-4 treatments (Spearman rho=+0.079, p<1e-69); the trend is essentially explained by inclusion of palbociclib in multi-treatment patients.

analyses.code: Welch t-test ER+ palbo+tam vs palbo alone (n=3,675 vs 8,519)
analyses.result_summary: PFS 5.90 vs 5.89 — adding tamoxifen on top of palbociclib confers no additional benefit (diff +0.013, p=0.83).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib benefit is greater at lower albumin (negative palbociclib*albumin interaction).
treatment_palbociclib benefit is smaller at higher ki67_pct (negative palbociclib*ki67 interaction).
treatment_palbociclib benefit is smaller at higher ldh_u_l (negative palbociclib*ldh interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit appears in advanced disease (positive interactions with stage_iv, has_brain_mets, msi_high).
After adjustment for clinical confounders, treatment_olaparib*brca_any interaction remains positive and may reach significance.

analyses.result_summary: Modest negative interaction: palbociclib:albumin coef=-0.047 (p=0.043); benefit slightly larger in patients with lower albumin.
Strong negative interaction: palbociclib:ki67 coef=-0.053 per Ki67% (p<1e-300). Each 10% rise in Ki67 erodes ~0.5 mo of palbociclib benefit.
Null (palbociclib:ldh coef=-6e-5, p=0.65).
All three interactions null (p=0.52, 0.58, 0.25 respectively); main effect remains slightly negative -0.044 (p=0.04). No clinical subgroup where pembrolizumab works.
Olaparib:brca_any coef=+0.124 (p=0.21); directional positive but underpowered with only 239 BRCA+ olaparib-treated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wild-type patients with low ki67_pct (<15%), treatment_palbociclib produces an extremely large PFS benefit (>3 months).
Within HR+/HER2- patients carrying pik3ca_mutation, treatment_palbociclib confers no PFS benefit (resistance phenotype).
Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wild-type patients with high ki67_pct (>=15%), treatment_palbociclib confers little PFS benefit.

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib in (er_positive==1 & her2_positive==0 & pik3ca_mutation==0 & ki67_pct<15)
analyses.result_summary: n=11,490. Palbociclib raises PFS by +4.69 months (8.97 vs 4.29), with consistent ~+4.7 mo benefit across all three age tertiles (young 7.01 vs 2.35; mid 8.98 vs 4.30; old 10.99 vs 6.24).

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib in (er_positive==1 & her2_positive==0 & pik3ca_mutation==1)
analyses.result_summary: n=9,908. Palbociclib effect 4.36 vs 4.33 (diff +0.022, p=0.62) — no benefit.

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib in (er_positive==1 & her2_positive==0 & pik3ca_mutation==0 & ki67_pct>=15)
analyses.result_summary: n=7,191. Palbociclib effect 4.34 vs 4.32 (diff +0.015, p=0.78) — no benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A comprehensive OLS with palbociclib*ER, palbociclib*HER2, palbociclib*PIK3CA, pembrolizumab*MSI, olaparib*BRCAany, and the major clinical/biomarker main effects fits the data well (R²>0.80) and confirms that palbociclib's interaction structure dominates while other treatment*biomarker interactions are NS.

When palbociclib interactions are included, the apparent main effects of er_positive, her2_positive, and pik3ca_mutation on PFS shrink to near zero, indicating these biomarkers are predictive (of palbociclib response) rather than prognostic.

analyses.code: Comprehensive OLS with main effects + palbociclib*ER + palbociclib*HER2 + palbociclib*PIK3CA + pembrolizumab*MSI + olaparib*BRCAany
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.843. Significant: age +0.176, ecog -1.18, stage_iv -1.55, brain_mets -0.97, albumin +0.48, ldh -5e-4, weight_loss -0.076, ki67 -0.019, palbociclib +0.84, palbociclib:ER +1.54, palbociclib:HER2+ -1.32, palbociclib:PIK3CA -1.65 (all p<1e-50). Main effects of ER (-0.003 p=0.78), HER2+ (-0.0005 p=0.97), PIK3CA (-0.001 p=0.93), MSI, BRCAany all collapse to NS. Pembrolizumab:MSI (+0.12 p=0.31), olaparib:BRCAany (+0.015 p=0.83), tamoxifen (+0.006 p=0.56), trastuzumab (-0.013 p=0.24), sacituzumab (+0.015 p=0.30) all NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the no-palbociclib subset, er_positive, her2_positive, and pik3ca_mutation have NO main-effect association with pfs_months — confirming they are predictive of palbociclib response but not prognostic.
The huge positive age-PFS association (+0.18 mo per year) is robust to multivariable adjustment for ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, ki67, weight loss, and palbociclib.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of biomarker main effects in palbociclib==0 subset (n=32,572)
analyses.result_summary: ER+ vs ER- diff=+0.011 (p=0.68); HER2+ vs HER2- diff=+0.015 (p=0.64); PIK3CA-mut vs wt diff=+0.008 (p=0.74); postmenopausal +0.031 (p=0.20); HER2-low -0.028 (p=0.25); BRCA1 +0.008 (p=0.91); BRCA2 -0.085 (p=0.27). All NS. Confirms these are pure predictive (palbociclib-modifying) biomarkers, not prognostic.
Adjusted age coef = +0.176 mo/yr (p<1e-300), basically unchanged from the unadjusted +0.18. Decile means rise monotonically from 1.7 mo (age 30-52) to 7.8 mo (age 78-90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the fully untreated cohort (none of the 6 treatments), the prognostic signal of age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin, LDH, and weight_loss is preserved while biomarker main effects (ER, HER2, PIK3CA) and ki67 effectively vanish — so the apparent ki67-PFS correlation in the full cohort is largely driven by palbociclib*ki67 interaction dilution.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + brain_mets + albumin + LDH + weight_loss + ki67 + ER + HER2 + PIK3CA on 12,478 fully-untreated patients
analyses.result_summary: Clinical predictors retain large effects (age +0.177, ecog -1.17, stage_iv -1.56, brain_mets -0.97, albumin +0.49, LDH -5.2e-4, weight_loss -0.078). Ki67 collapses to -0.0002 (p=0.094). ER, HER2, PIK3CA all NS — confirming they are non-prognostic in untreated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab provides PFS benefit in patients with msi_high OR BRCA1/2 mutations (combined immunotherapy-sensitive proxy).
Among palbociclib-treated patients, pfs_months differs by sex_female.

analyses.result_summary: PFS 4.59 vs 4.65 months (diff -0.057, p=0.64). No benefit in this composite subgroup.
Female palbo PFS 5.40 vs male palbo PFS 5.42 (p=0.68). Despite the cohort being 55% male — atypical for a 'breast' cohort — palbociclib effect is identical by sex.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within HR+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/low-Ki67 patients, palbociclib benefit is comparable across age tertiles (~+4.7 months in each).
A parsimonious model with age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin, LDH, weight_loss, palbociclib, and palbociclib*{ER, HER2, PIK3CA, ki67} interactions captures >85% of PFS variance.

analyses.code: Welch t-test palbociclib in age tertiles within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt/Ki67<15
analyses.result_summary: Young (30-60 yr): tx 7.01 vs 2.35 (+4.66, p<1e-300). Mid (60-69 yr): 8.98 vs 4.30 (+4.68). Old (70-90 yr): 10.99 vs 6.24 (+4.75). Absolute benefit very stable across ages despite large baseline age effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ age + ecog + stage_iv + brain_mets + albumin + LDH + weight_loss + palbociclib*(er + her2 + pik3ca + ki67)
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.858. All key coefficients significant at p<1e-20: age +0.176, ecog -1.18, stage_iv -1.55, brain_mets -0.97, albumin +0.48, LDH -5e-4, weight_loss -0.076, palbociclib +1.66, palbociclib:ER +1.53, palbociclib:HER2+ -1.32, palbociclib:PIK3CA -1.64, palbociclib:ki67 -0.053. No SNP, comorbidity, demographic, symptom, or treatment-other-than-palbociclib variables needed.
